question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BlockStreamVerifier.verifyWrappedRecordBlockSignature() method's handling of unknown node IDs where Utility.handleRecoverableError() is called and the signature is skipped rather than failing hard, by crafting a wrapped record block where all signatures reference non-existent node IDs, causing the signatures list to be empty and potentially bypassing the nodeSignatureVerifier.verify() threshold check?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BatchStreamFileNotifier.StreamFileSubscriber's unsynchronized buffer (ArrayList) which is documented as 'Un-synchronized since only one thread reads and writes from it', by triggering a scenario where the executorService shutdown races with a buffer flush, causing a ConcurrentModificationException that corrupts the buffer state and leads to stream files being parsed out of order or skipped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Downloader.verifyHash() method's use of Objects.equals() for comparing fileHash and metadataHash strings, where if both actual and expected are null (e.g., when getFileHash() or getMetadataHashAsHex() returns null), Objects.equals(null, null) returns true, bypassing the file and metadata hash checks and allowing a tampered stream file to be forwarded to streamFileNotifier.verified()?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BlockStreamVerifier.verifyHashChain() transition logic where the first block after cutover (hasRecordFile() == false, last.getVersion() < BlockStreamReader.VERSION) sets blockFile.setPreviousWrappedRecordBlockHash(Hex.decode(blockFile.getPreviousHash())) without verifying the previousHash against the actual last record file hash, allowing an attacker to inject a block with an arbitrary previousHash that becomes the anchor for all subsequent wrapped record block hash chain verification?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Downloader.downloadNextBatch() exception handling where SignatureVerificationException is caught and only logged as a warning while other exceptions are logged as errors, by crafting a stream of files that repeatedly trigger SignatureVerificationException, causing the downloader to continuously warn and retry without ever successfully ingesting legitimate Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Downloader.verifySigsAndDownloadDataFiles() logic where if a SignatureVerificationException is thrown for a non-last signature group, the loop continues to the next group with 'Trying next group', by crafting a sequence of invalid signature groups followed by a tampered but valid-looking group, causing the downloader to skip legitimate files and accept the tampered group's data file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BatchStreamFileNotifier.StreamFileSubscriber.handle() method's null check for streamFile (handling the timeout/poll case), by causing the queue to return null repeatedly via timing manipulation, triggering repeated flushes of a partially-filled buffer with incomplete Hashgraph history data that gets parsed prematurely?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Downloader.verifySignatures() method's fallback behavior where FileOperationException, HashMismatchException, and TransientProviderException are caught and logged as warnings while the loop continues to the next signature, by crafting a scenario where all CONSENSUS_REACHED signatures correspond to nodes that return hash mismatches, causing verifySignatures() to return false and no file to be forwarded to streamFileNotifier.verified(), creating a history gap?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BlockStreamVerifier.verify() method's finally block which always records the streamVerificationMeterProvider metric regardless of success or failure, by flooding the system with invalid blocks that all fail verification, causing metric cardinality explosion (due to per-node tags) that exhausts memory and prevents legitimate block verification from completing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Downloader.setStreamFileIndex() method which uses lastStreamFile.get().map(StreamFile::getIndex).map(v -> v + 1) to assign sequential indices, by causing lastStreamFile to hold a StreamFile with a manipulated getIndex() value (e.g., Long.MAX_VALUE), triggering an arithmetic overflow that wraps the index to Long.MIN_VALUE and corrupts the block index sequence in the mirror node database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BlockStreamVerifier.verifyHashChain() method's version comparison (last.getVersion() < BlockStreamReader.VERSION) to determine which hash chain path is taken, by injecting a record file with a manipulated version field that causes the wrong hash chain verification branch to execute, allowing a tampered block to bypass the appropriate hash chain check before streamFileNotifier.verified() is called?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Downloader.downloadAndParseSigFiles() method's use of Flux.fromIterable(nodes).flatMap() with onErrorContinue(), where errors downloading signature files for individual nodes are silently swallowed, by causing errors for all honest nodes while providing valid-looking signatures from attacker-controlled nodes, reducing the effective consensus pool and making it easier to achieve CONSENSUS_REACHED for tampered files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BatchStreamFileNotifier.StreamFileSubscriber's flush() method which checks maxFiles, maxItems, and flushInterval conditions, by submitting stream files with manipulated getCount() values (returning Long.MAX_VALUE), causing the maxItems accumulation to overflow and trigger premature or missed flushes that corrupt the batch parsing order of Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Downloader.onVerified() method's lastStreamFile.set(Optional.of(copy)) update which happens after streamFileNotifier.verified() is called, such that in a concurrent scenario another thread reads the stale lastStreamFile before the update, uses the old hash as expectedPrevHash, and accepts a tampered file whose previousHash matches the old (not current) chain tip?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BlockStreamVerifier.verifyTssSignature() method's logTssSignatureSize flag which is a non-volatile, non-synchronized boolean field, by triggering a data race where multiple threads read logTssSignatureSize as true simultaneously, causing duplicate log entries that could be used to infer internal block processing state and time attacks against the TSS signature verification?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Downloader.verifySignatures() method's endDate check (dataFilename.getInstant().isAfter(endDate)) which disables the downloader when a file's timestamp exceeds endDate, by crafting a stream file with a far-future timestamp that causes downloaderProperties.setEnabled(false) to be called, permanently halting all future Hashgraph history ingestion without any recovery mechanism?
